text:

Ubisoft seems to have another leak problem, as what appears to be a remastered version of Rayman Origins has appeared on the Xbox Store – and it's only $2,000.

The Stop Killing Games movement has reached yet another major milestone after the California State Assembly passed a bill protecting games from being pulled offline.

While Disney+ hasn’t announced anything new from the worlds of the MCU or Star Wars, there are still plenty of big additions coming to the service in June.

text:
Minecraft Live May 2026: Everything Announced
Mojang Studios showed up for its Minecraft Live May 2026 presentation with reveals for A Minecraft Movie 2, Minecraft Dungeons 2, and more, and we were there to gather all of the highlights.

text:
Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Devs Say 'Keep the Receipts' Amid Commitment to Grounded Skins
Developers at Activision and Infinity Ward have told fans to "keep the receipts" after promising to remain grounded regarding cosmetics in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4.

text:
7 Reasons Why the AC Black Flag Remake Could be Amazing (and 3 Concerns)
Black Flag Resynced looks amazing, but with the glaring omission of its DLC expansion and modern day story, it could fall short of being a definitive experience.

We chat with Punisher vs. Spider-Man writer Dan Abnett to learn how the series will explore the collapse of what was once a beautiful friendship.

text:
Bungie Thanks Fans With First Look at Monumental Destiny 2 Update
Monument of Triumph will be Bungie's last major content update for Destiny 2, but that isn't stopping the studio from launching what some fans think will be its "best singular update in years."

author: IGN RSS
text:
New Xbox Boss Says Decision to Continue to Show PS5 Logos During Its Xbox Games Showcase 'Was a Miss, and I Own It'
Xbox boss Asha Sharma has reacted to negative feedback at Microsoft’s decision to continue to show competing platform logos at its upcoming Xbox Games Showcase, suggesting it will stop doing so for future shows.

author: IGN RSS
text:
Marcia Lucas, Oscar-Winning Star Wars and Return of the Jedi Editor, Dies at 80
Marcia Lucas, the film editor who won an Academy Award for Best Film Editing for Star Wars and was the ex-wife of Star Wars creator George Lucas, has died at age 80.

text:

Star City lead Rhys Ifans and showrunners Matt Wolpert and Ben Nedivi explain why it's time to tell the Russians' side of the alt-history story.

text:
Toy Story 5 Has 'One of the Most Heartbreaking Scenes' in the Franchise, Tom Hanks Says
Longtime Woody actor Tom Hanks has said Toy Story 5 has "one of the most heartbreaking scenes" the Pixar movie franchise has seen in its 30-year history.
